get.description: Gets the information about the calling user. If the caller is not authenticated, returns `null` as the `user`.
Finds a profile by its handle, ID, or Hackatime ID.

schema.description: The ID of the profile to query. Can be undefined if another field is specified.
The handle of the profile to query. Can be undefined if another field is specified.
The Hackatime ID of the profile to query. Can be undefined if another field is specified.

get.description: Finds a profile by its e-mail. Only administrators and program keys can call this endpoint.

schema.description: The case-sensitive e-mail of the user to look up.

user.description: The found user. `null` if no user exists with that -email

id.description: The ID of the target user to edit. If the calling user has their permissionLevel set to 'USER', this field can only be set to their ID.

changes.description: The changes to apply to the user profile.

user.description: The new state of the user, after applying the updates.

get.description: Gets all devices registered by the currently authenticated user.

post.description: Creates a new device owned by a user, allocating a new, unique ID.

name.description: The initial string to use as the user-friendly device display name.

delete.description: Removes a device owned by a user.

id.description: The ID of the device to remove. The device must be owned by the calling user.

post.description: Signs out the current user by clearing the authentication cookie.

get.description: Gets a list of Hackatime projects that have been associated with the user's timelapses, including the total hour counts.

time.description: The amount of time spent timelapsing.

projects.description: All of the Hackatime projects associated with timelapses.

get.description: Queries the aggregate duration of all timelapses of a given user.

schema.description: The ID of the user to query the total timelapse time of. If `null`, and the user is authenticated, the user's ID is used instead.

time.description: The aggregate duration of all timelapses of the queried user.

post.description: Updates the last heartbeat time of the calling user to the current date. This is used to detect active users.
Creates a request for a specific device to relay its key to the calling device. Only one key relay request can exist at one time.

targetDevice.description: The device ID the client is interested in receiving the key for.

callingDevice.description: The device ID that is calling this endpoint.

exchangeId.description: The exchange request UUID. This should be used to poll the status of the exchange with `receiveKeyRelay`. If `null`, another key exchange is in progress with the target device.

get.description: Queries the current key relay request to the calling device, if one exists.

schema.description: The device to fetch the current requests for.

exchangeId.description: The exchange request UUID to use with `provideKeyRelay` or `denyKeyRelay`.

callingDevice.description: The device ID that requests the exchange.

request.description: The current request. If `null`, no request is pending currently.

post.description: Provides a device key for relay transfer to another device. The key is stored in-memory with a short TTL, and can only be retrieved once via `receiveKeyRelay`.

exchangeId.description: The exchange request UUID to fulfill.

deviceKey.description: The 128-bit device key in lowercase hex.

post.description: Denies a request for relay transfer to another device.
Receives a device key previously relayed with `provideKeyRelay`. Consumes the relay entry on success.

exchangeId.description: The exchange request UUID, received from `requestKeyRelay`.

relay.description: The relayed device key, or `null` if no relay is pending.

get.description: Finds a timelapse by its ID. This endpoint will return a different view if the user owns the timelapse.

schema.description: The ID of the timelapse to query information about.

id.description: The ID of the timelapse.

createdAt.description: The date when the timelapse was created.

owner.description: Information about the owner/author of the timelapse.

playbackUrl.description: The public URL that can be used to stream video data. If `null`, the timelapse is still being processed.

thumbnailUrl.description: The URL of the thumbnail image for this timelapse. If `null`, the timelapse is still being processed. It's recommended to derive the processing status of the timelapse from `playbackUrl`.

duration.description: The duration of the timelapse, in seconds. Must be non-negative.

isDraft.description: Always `false`. This field is provided for convenience when using strongly-typed clients.

post.description: Publishes a draft timelapse. This will delete the draft timelapse.

id.description: The ID of the draft timelapse to publish.

visibility.description: The visibility the published timelapse should have. This can be changed later.

deviceKey.description: The 128-bit device key (lowercase hex) used to decrypt the video sessions in the draft timelapse.

hackatimeProject.description: If provided, the timelapse snapshots will be synced to this Hackatime project after processing completes.

id.description: The ID of the timelapse.

createdAt.description: The date when the timelapse was created.

owner.description: Information about the owner/author of the timelapse.

playbackUrl.description: The public URL that can be used to stream video data. If `null`, the timelapse is still being processed.

thumbnailUrl.description: The URL of the thumbnail image for this timelapse. If `null`, the timelapse is still being processed. It's recommended to derive the processing status of the timelapse from `playbackUrl`.

duration.description: The duration of the timelapse, in seconds. Must be non-negative.

isDraft.description: Always `false`. This field is provided for convenience when using strongly-typed clients.

patch.description: Updates the metadata of a timelapse.

id.description: The ID of the timelapse to update.

changes.description: The changes to apply to the timelapse.

id.description: The ID of the timelapse.

createdAt.description: The date when the timelapse was created.

owner.description: Information about the owner/author of the timelapse.

playbackUrl.description: The public URL that can be used to stream video data. If `null`, the timelapse is still being processed.

thumbnailUrl.description: The URL of the thumbnail image for this timelapse. If `null`, the timelapse is still being processed. It's recommended to derive the processing status of the timelapse from `playbackUrl`.

duration.description: The duration of the timelapse, in seconds. Must be non-negative.

isDraft.description: Always `false`. This field is provided for convenience when using strongly-typed clients.

timelapse.description: The new state of the timelapse, after applying the updates.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a timelapse owned by the user.

get.description: Finds all timelapses created by a given user.

id.description: The ID of the timelapse.

createdAt.description: The date when the timelapse was created.

owner.description: Information about the owner/author of the timelapse.

playbackUrl.description: The public URL that can be used to stream video data. If `null`, the timelapse is still being processed.

thumbnailUrl.description: The URL of the thumbnail image for this timelapse. If `null`, the timelapse is still being processed. It's recommended to derive the processing status of the timelapse from `playbackUrl`.

duration.description: The duration of the timelapse, in seconds. Must be non-negative.

isDraft.description: Always `false`. This field is provided for convenience when using strongly-typed clients.

timelapses.description: All timelapses created by the user.

post.description: Synchronizes a timelapse with a Hackatime project, converting all snapshots into heartbeats. This procedure can only be called once for a timelapse.

id.description: The ID of the timelapse.

createdAt.description: The date when the timelapse was created.

owner.description: Information about the owner/author of the timelapse.

playbackUrl.description: The public URL that can be used to stream video data. If `null`, the timelapse is still being processed.

thumbnailUrl.description: The URL of the thumbnail image for this timelapse. If `null`, the timelapse is still being processed. It's recommended to derive the processing status of the timelapse from `playbackUrl`.

duration.description: The duration of the timelapse, in seconds. Must be non-negative.

isDraft.description: Always `false`. This field is provided for convenience when using strongly-typed clients.

get.description: Lists all published timelapses owned by the authenticated user, with cursor-based pagination.

schema.description: The ID of the last timelapse from the previous page. Omit to start from the beginning.
The maximum number of timelapses to return per page.

id.description: The ID of the timelapse.

createdAt.description: The date when the timelapse was created.

owner.description: Information about the owner/author of the timelapse.

playbackUrl.description: The public URL that can be used to stream video data. If `null`, the timelapse is still being processed.

thumbnailUrl.description: The URL of the thumbnail image for this timelapse. If `null`, the timelapse is still being processed. It's recommended to derive the processing status of the timelapse from `playbackUrl`.

duration.description: The duration of the timelapse, in seconds. Must be non-negative.

isDraft.description: Always `false`. This field is provided for convenience when using strongly-typed clients.

nextCursor.description: The cursor to use for the next page. `null` if there are no more results.

get.description: Gets all draft timelapses created by the given user.

schema.description: The ID of the user to fetch the draft timelapses for. For regular users, this must be the ID of the authenticated user.

name.description: The current name of the draft timelapse.

description.description: The current description of the draft timelapse.

begin.description: The start time (in seconds) of the region this entry describes.

end.description: The end time (in seconds) of the region this entry describes. This field has to be greater than `begin`.

kind.description: The kind of edit to apply.

editList.description: The edits to apply to the resulting footage.

id.description: The ID of the draft timelapse. This will *not* be the same as the ID of the resulting published timelapse.

sessions.description: URIs pointing to separate, sequential video files that compose the timelapse.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the draft timelapse was created.

previewThumbnail.description: An URL to an encrypted user-generated preview thumbnail that represents the timelapse.

deviceId.description: The UUID of the known device that has encrypted this draft.

owner.description: The user that has created this draft timelapse.

isDraft.description: Always `true`. This field is provided for convenience when using strongly-typed clients.

iv.description: The IV to use when encrypting and decrypting binary data associated with the timelapse.

associatedTimelapseId.description: If defined, this draft is currently being published and has an associated timelapse that is processing.

get.description: Finds a draft timelapse by its ID. If the caller is not the owner of the draft or an admin, this endpoint will report that the timelapse doesn't exist.

schema.description: The ID of the draft timelapse.

name.description: The current name of the draft timelapse.

description.description: The current description of the draft timelapse.

begin.description: The start time (in seconds) of the region this entry describes.

end.description: The end time (in seconds) of the region this entry describes. This field has to be greater than `begin`.

kind.description: The kind of edit to apply.

editList.description: The edits to apply to the resulting footage.

id.description: The ID of the draft timelapse. This will *not* be the same as the ID of the resulting published timelapse.

sessions.description: URIs pointing to separate, sequential video files that compose the timelapse.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the draft timelapse was created.

previewThumbnail.description: An URL to an encrypted user-generated preview thumbnail that represents the timelapse.

deviceId.description: The UUID of the known device that has encrypted this draft.

owner.description: The user that has created this draft timelapse.

isDraft.description: Always `true`. This field is provided for convenience when using strongly-typed clients.

iv.description: The IV to use when encrypting and decrypting binary data associated with the timelapse.

associatedTimelapseId.description: If defined, this draft is currently being published and has an associated timelapse that is processing.

post.description: Creates a draft timelapse. Draft timelapses can be published and turned into regular timelapses by calling "timelapse.publish".

name.description: The name to assign to the created timelapse draft.

description.description: The description to assign to the created timelapse draft.

snapshots.description: An array of timestamps. Each timestamp counts the number of milliseconds since the Unix epoch - equivalent to `Date.getTime()` in JavaScript. The frame count is inferred by sorting the array, and always begins at 0.

deviceId.description: The device that the timelapse has been created on. This is used to let other devices know which key to use to decrypt this timelapse.

fileSize.description:
The size, in bytes, of this session. The session is expected to be *exactly* this
                            large - the PUT upload URL for this session will enforce the `Content-Length`
                            HTTP header to be equal to this value.

sessions.description:
The sessions that make up this draft. A session is a separately recorded video stream - all sessions are concatenated
                together into one master video during server re-encoding.

thumbnailSize.description: The size, in bytes, of the user-generated preview thumbnail for this draft. As is the case for sessions, the size of the thumbnail is expected to match this value exactly.

name.description: The current name of the draft timelapse.

description.description: The current description of the draft timelapse.

begin.description: The start time (in seconds) of the region this entry describes.

end.description: The end time (in seconds) of the region this entry describes. This field has to be greater than `begin`.

kind.description: The kind of edit to apply.

editList.description: The edits to apply to the resulting footage.

id.description: The ID of the draft timelapse. This will *not* be the same as the ID of the resulting published timelapse.

sessions.description: URIs pointing to separate, sequential video files that compose the timelapse.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the draft timelapse was created.

previewThumbnail.description: An URL to an encrypted user-generated preview thumbnail that represents the timelapse.

deviceId.description: The UUID of the known device that has encrypted this draft.

owner.description: The user that has created this draft timelapse.

isDraft.description: Always `true`. This field is provided for convenience when using strongly-typed clients.

iv.description: The IV to use when encrypting and decrypting binary data associated with the timelapse.

associatedTimelapseId.description: If defined, this draft is currently being published and has an associated timelapse that is processing.

sessionUploadTokens.description: An array containing the upload JWT tokens to pass over as the tus metadata when uploading the sessions.

thumbnailUploadToken.description: The `tus` upload JWT token identifying the encrypted preview thumbnail. This thumbnail will *not* be used when publishing, and will be encrypted with the device's passkey.

id.description: The ID of the draft timelapse to update.

name.description: The current name of the draft timelapse.

description.description: The current description of the draft timelapse.

begin.description: The start time (in seconds) of the region this entry describes.

end.description: The end time (in seconds) of the region this entry describes. This field has to be greater than `begin`.

kind.description: The kind of edit to apply.

editList.description: The edits to apply to the resulting footage.

changes.description: The changes to apply to the draft timelapse.

name.description: The current name of the draft timelapse.

description.description: The current description of the draft timelapse.

begin.description: The start time (in seconds) of the region this entry describes.

end.description: The end time (in seconds) of the region this entry describes. This field has to be greater than `begin`.

kind.description: The kind of edit to apply.

editList.description: The edits to apply to the resulting footage.

id.description: The ID of the draft timelapse. This will *not* be the same as the ID of the resulting published timelapse.

sessions.description: URIs pointing to separate, sequential video files that compose the timelapse.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the draft timelapse was created.

previewThumbnail.description: An URL to an encrypted user-generated preview thumbnail that represents the timelapse.

deviceId.description: The UUID of the known device that has encrypted this draft.

owner.description: The user that has created this draft timelapse.

isDraft.description: Always `true`. This field is provided for convenience when using strongly-typed clients.

iv.description: The IV to use when encrypting and decrypting binary data associated with the timelapse.

associatedTimelapseId.description: If defined, this draft is currently being published and has an associated timelapse that is processing.

timelapse.description: The new state of the draft timelapse, after applying the updates.

delete.description: Permanently deletes a draft timelapse owned by the user.

get.description: Queries all unpublished timelapses that were created by the caller with a legacy version of Lapse that have not yet been migrated.

id.description: The original ID of the unpublished timelapse.

name.description: The original name (title) of the timelapse.

description.description: The original description of the timelapse.

primarySession.description: A URL pointing to the encrypted primary session of the timelapse. Legacy versions of Lapse only supported single-session timelapses.

thumbnailUrl.description: A URL pointing to the encrypted thumbnail URL of the timelapse.

deviceId.description: The ID of the device that has created this timelapse.

snapshots.description: Timestamps taken at regular intervals while the timelapse was recorded. This directly represents the resulting Hackatime heartbeats.

timelapses.description: The legacy timelapses created by the user that have not yet been migrated.

post.description: Marks a legacy unpublished timelapse as migrated. This does not delete the record - it only prevents it from appearing in the legacy query.

id.description: The ID of the legacy unpublished timelapse to mark as migrated.

post.description: Creates a new comment for the given timelapse.

id.description: The ID of the timelapse this comment should be created for.

content.description: The content of the comment. This can contain a limited subset of Markdown (bold, italic, strikethrough, code).

delete.description: Deletes a comment owned by the calling user.

commentId.description: The ID of the comment to delete.

post.description: Rotates the secret for an OAuth app owned by the calling user.

id.description: The app ID to rotate the secret for.

clientSecret.description: The new client secret assigned to the app.

post.description: Updates data associated with an OAuth app by its ID. Regular users may update apps they own; administrators may update any app.

id.description: The app ID to update the information of.

post.description: Revokes an OAuth app owned by the calling user, permanently disallowing any user from authenticating through it.

id.description: The ID of the app to revoke.

post.description: Gets all OAuth apps owned by the calling user.
Creates a new OAuth app.

clientSecret.description: The client secret for the app - this is not stored on the server, and will only be returned with this request or when rotating.

post.description: Gets all OAuth apps created by any user. This procedure requires administrator access.
Updates the trust level of an OAuth app. This procedure requires administrator access.

id.description: The app ID to update.

post.description: Gets all OAuth grants for the authenticated user.
Revokes an OAuth grant for the authenticated user.

grantId.description: The ID of the grant to revoke.

get.description: Looks up a public summary of an OAuth app by its OAuth2 client ID. This is used by the consent screen and does not require authentication.

schema.description: The OAuth2 client ID to look up.

get.description: Returns the users that have the most Lapse time logged in the past 7 days.
Returns the most recent public timelapses at the time of the API call.

id.description: The ID of the timelapse.

createdAt.description: The date when the timelapse was created.

owner.description: Information about the owner/author of the timelapse.

playbackUrl.description: The public URL that can be used to stream video data. If `null`, the timelapse is still being processed.

thumbnailUrl.description: The URL of the thumbnail image for this timelapse. If `null`, the timelapse is still being processed. It's recommended to derive the processing status of the timelapse from `playbackUrl`.

duration.description: The duration of the timelapse, in seconds. Must be non-negative.

isDraft.description: Always `false`. This field is provided for convenience when using strongly-typed clients.

get.description: Returns the number of active users that have sent a heartbeat in the last 60s.

post.description: Gets all Hackatime projects from the user's Hackatime account.

get.description: Gets the authenticated user's public and unlisted timelapses associated with the given Hackatime project key.

schema.description: The exact, case-sensitive Hackatime project key to query.

count.description: The number of timelapses made by the authenticated user associated with the project key.

id.description: The ID of the timelapse.

createdAt.description: The date when the timelapse was created.

owner.description: Information about the owner/author of the timelapse.

playbackUrl.description: The public URL that can be used to stream video data. If `null`, the timelapse is still being processed.

thumbnailUrl.description: The URL of the thumbnail image for this timelapse. If `null`, the timelapse is still being processed. It's recommended to derive the processing status of the timelapse from `playbackUrl`.

duration.description: The duration of the timelapse, in seconds. Must be non-negative.

isDraft.description: Always `false`. This field is provided for convenience when using strongly-typed clients.

timelapses.description: The timelapses made by the authenticated user associated with the project key.

get.description: Gets the timelapses of a given Hackatime user associated with the given Hackatime project key.

schema.description: The Hackatime user ID of the Lapse user that should be the subject of this API call.
The exact, case-sensitive Hackatime project key to query.

count.description:
The number of timelapses made by the user associated with the project key. This number may be greater than `timelapses`
                    if the API request was unauthenticated and the user has unlisted timelapses associated with the key.

id.description: The ID of the timelapse.

createdAt.description: The date when the timelapse was created.

owner.description: Information about the owner/author of the timelapse.

playbackUrl.description: The public URL that can be used to stream video data. If `null`, the timelapse is still being processed.

thumbnailUrl.description: The URL of the thumbnail image for this timelapse. If `null`, the timelapse is still being processed. It's recommended to derive the processing status of the timelapse from `playbackUrl`.

duration.description: The duration of the timelapse, in seconds. Must be non-negative.

isDraft.description: Always `false`. This field is provided for convenience when using strongly-typed clients.

timelapses.description: The timelapses made by the user associated with the project key.

get.description:
Initiates the regular OAuth2 flow for Lapse authentication, possibly asking the user to log in via Hackatime, and, for non-canonical
                (third-party) clients, asking for consent.

                **This is a user-agent endpoint.** Clients are expected to open it in a browser (or browser-equivalent) environment - that is,
                it will redirect the user to a user-facing HTML/CSS/JS webpage. It should NOT be queried directly.

post.description: Generates a token that represents approved consent to a pending OAuth2 request. **This can only be called when authenticated with the `elevated` scope, which is only available to the canonical app.**

clientId.description: The client ID to issue the consent token for.

state.description: The state variable, to prevent CSRF attacks.

scopes.description: The scopes to grant access to.

token.description: The token that should be passed to /auth/continue in order to continue the OAuth2 authorization flow.

post.description: Exchanges an authorization code for an access token.

get.description: Returns aggregate statistics for the admin dashboard. Requires administrator permissions and an `elevated` grant.

post.description: Lists entities with filtering, sorting, and pagination. Requires administrator permissions and an `elevated` grant.

patch.description: Updates a single entity record. Requires administrator permissions and an `elevated` grant. Permission level changes require ROOT.

post.description: Fuzzy search across all entities. Requires administrator permissions and an `elevated` grant.
Exports the raw database record for the given entity. Requires administrator permissions and an `elevated` grant.
Creates a new program key. The raw key is only returned with this response or when rotating. Requires ROOT access.

name.description: A human-readable name for the key.

scopes.description: The scopes to grant to this key.

expiresAt.description: Expiration timestamp in milliseconds since Unix epoch. Must be within 1 year from now.

rawKey.description: The raw program key — this will only be shown once.

post.description: Lists all program keys. Requires ROOT access.
Rotates a program key, generating a new raw key. The old key is immediately invalidated. Requires ROOT access.

id.description: The ID of the program key to rotate.

rawKey.description: The new raw program key — this will only be shown once.

post.description: Revokes a program key, permanently disabling it. Requires ROOT access.

id.description: The ID of the program key to revoke.

post.description: Updates the name and/or scopes of an existing program key. Requires ROOT access.

id.description: The ID of the program key to update.

name.description: The new name for this key.

scopes.description: The new set of scopes for this key.
